name: Angkor Wat
summary: Angkor Wat is a temple complex in Cambodia and the largest religious monument in the world. Originally constructed as a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu by the Khmer King Suryavarman II in the early 12th century, it gradually transformed into a Buddhist temple by the end of the century. The temple is a symbol of Cambodia and appears on the national flag.

timeline.title: Construction Begins
timeline.description: Khmer King Suryavarman II began construction of Angkor Wat as a state temple dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. The temple was designed to represent Mount Meru, the home of the gods in Hindu mythology, with its five central towers symbolizing the peaks of the sacred mountain.

timeline.title: Construction Completed
timeline.description: The main temple complex was completed after approximately 37 years of construction. Angkor Wat became the largest religious monument in the world, covering an area of 162.6 hectares with an outer moat measuring 3.6 kilometers.

timeline.title: Champa Invasion
timeline.description: The Champa kingdom invaded and sacked Angkor, leading to political upheaval. King Jayavarman VII eventually recaptured the capital and established a new city, Angkor Thom, nearby.

timeline.title: Conversion to Buddhism
timeline.description: Angkor Wat gradually transformed from a Hindu temple to a Buddhist site under King Jayavarman VII, who built numerous Buddhist temples throughout the Angkor region. Buddhist sculptures and modifications were added to the existing Hindu architecture.

timeline.title: Abandonment of Angkor
timeline.description: The Thai kingdom of Ayutthaya invaded and conquered Angkor, leading to the abandonment of the capital. Angkor Wat was gradually reclaimed by the jungle, though it was never completely abandoned by Buddhist monks.

timeline.title: Rediscovery by the West
timeline.description: French explorer Henri Mouhot rediscovered Angkor Wat and brought it to international attention through his published accounts. His writings sparked European interest in the ancient Khmer civilization.

timeline.title: UNESCO World Heritage Site
timeline.description: Angkor Wat and the entire Angkor archaeological park were designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognizing the outstanding universal value of the Khmer architectural and artistic heritage.
